Question: Determine whether the ordered pair $$( 8 , 5 )$$ is a solution to the following system. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}5 x -4 y = 20 \\ 2 x + 1 = 3 y\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the following system of equations by graphing. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x - 5 y = -25 \\ - 4 x + 5 y = 35\end{matrix}\right .$$
Answer: The solution to the system is the ordered pair $$( -5 , 3 ) .$$

Question: Solve the following system of equations by substitution. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}x = y + 3 \\ 4 = 3 x -2 y\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system of equations by addition. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 x -7 y = 2 \\ 3 x + y = -20\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the system of equations by addition. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}2 x + 3 y & = & 8 \\ 3 x + 5 y & = & 10\end{matrix}\right .$$
Solve the following system of equations in two variables. $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}2 y -2 x = 2 \\ 2 y -2 x = 6\end{matrix}\right .$$
Answer: No solution. It is an inconsistent system.

Question: Solve the following system of equations in two variables. $$None$$
Answer: The system is dependent so there are infinite solutions of the form $$( x , 2 x + 5 ) .$$

Question: Meal tickets at the circus cost $$\text{$} 4.00$$ for children and $$\text{$} 12.00$$ for adults. If $$1,650$$ meal tickets were bought for a total of $$\text{$} 14,200 ,$$ how many children and how many adults bought meal tickets?
